dtcs.description: High Speed CAN Communication Bus off A communication line exists between the Engine Control Module(PCM) and the Transaxle Control Module(TCM). The communication is through a Control Area Network(CAN). Without CAN communication, an independent pin and wiring is needed to receive a sensor information from a PCM. The more information to be communicated, the more wirings is required. In case of CAN communication type, all the information need to be communicated among control modules such as PCM and ABS control module use CAN lines. The PCM determines CAN communication error and sets DTC U0001 if communication with other control devices (e.g. ABS) via CAN is impossible or PCM detects that communication time via CAN exceeds threshold value. DTC Detecting Condition Diagnostic Circuit Diagram Engine Control

steps.instruction: Connect GDS and select "DTC Analysis" mode.
Click "DTC Status" on the menu bar to see DTC's information.
Confirm that "DTC Readiness Flag" indicates "COMPLETED". If not, drive the vehicle within conditions noted in the freeze frame data or enable conditions.
Read "DTC Status" parameter.
Is parameter displayed "History(Not Present) fault"?

dtcs.description: Accelerator Pedal Position Signal Error CAN communication is a parallel circuit to interchange information among each units. CAN communication can share much information with units more faster and more efficiently by using specified language. CAN-High line is twisted with CAN-Low line as a purpose in preventing from a noise. The operating duty of EMC is maintained at 100 : 0 (2WD) for guaranteeing stabilized driving force, accuracy of the control during inside ABS, FTCS, ESP control. The 4WD ECM sets DTC U0100 if it doesn't receive APS signals from ECM. DTC Detecting Condition Diagnostic Circuit Diagram 4WD Control

steps.instruction: Connect scantool to Data Link Connector(DLC).
Self-diagnose 4WD ECM with scantool.
Check the change of the sensor's value as the throttle position sensor's value varies according to the change of accelerator.
If the sensor's value is stuck, self-diagnose ECM with scantool. So if there are any DTCs related to the sensor, inspect it at first and check if the DTCs have been erased.
In a case that there is no DTC, select "Accelerator pedal sensor" item among the service data.

Check the sensor's change according to the accelerator pedal's change. Specification :It varies according to the accelerator pedal's change. Fig.1) Throttle Position(4WD) Fig.2) Throttle Position(ECM)
Is parameter displayed within specifications? W/Harness Inspection 4WD Control

dtcs.description: Lost Communication With TCM A communication line exists between the Engine Control Module(PCM) and the Transaxle Control Module(TCM). The communication is through a Control Area Network(CAN). Without CAN communication, an independent pin and wiring is needed to receive a sensor information from a PCM. The more information to be communicated, the more wirings is required. In case of CAN communication type, all the information need to be communicated among control modules such as PCM and ABS control module use CAN lines. The PCM determines CAN communication error and sets DTC U0101 if no message received from TCM. DTC Detecting Condition Diagnostic Circuit Diagram Engine Control

steps.instruction: Connect GDS and select "DTC Analysis" mode.
Click "DTC Status" on the menu bar to see DTC's information.
Confirm that "DTC Readiness Flag" indicates "COMPLETED". If not, drive the vehicle within conditions noted in the freeze frame data or enable conditions.
Read "DTC Status" parameter.
Is parameter displayed "History(Not Present) fault"?

dtcs.description: Lost Communication With Anti-Lock Brake System (ABS) Control Module CAN communication is a parallel circuit to interchange information among each units. CAN communication can share much information with units more faster and more efficiently by using specified language. CAN-High line is twisted with CAN-Low line as a purpose in preventing from a noise. The operating duty of EMC is maintained at 100 : 0 (2WD) for guaranteeing stabilized driving force, accuracy of the control during inside ABS, FTCS, ESP control.

steps.instruction: Connect scantool to Data Link Connector(DLC).
Self-diagnose 4WD ECM with scantool.
Check the change of each wheel speed sensor's value according to the change of vehicle speed.
If the sensor's value is stuck, self-diagnose ABS ECM with scantool. So if there are any DTCs related to the sensor, inspect it at first and check if the DTCs have been erased.
In a case that there is no DTC, select "RPM" item among the service data.
Check the change of the sensor's value according to a vehicle's speed. Specification :It varies according to a vehicle's speed. Fig.1) Wheel Speed Velocity(4WD) Fig.2) Wheel Speed Sensor(ABS)
Is parameter displayed within specifications? W/Harness Inspection 4WD Control

dtcs.description: Lost Communication With Vehicle Dynamics Control Module CAN communication is a parallel circuit to interchange information among each units. CAN communication can share much information with units more faster and more efficiently by using specified language. CAN-High line is twisted with CAN-Low line as a purpose in preventing from a noise. The operating duty of EMC is maintained at 100 : 0 (2WD) for guaranteeing stabilized driving force, accuracy of the control during inside ABS, FTCS, ESP control.

steps.instruction: Connect scantool to Data Link Connector(DLC).
Self-diagnose 4WD ECM with scantool.
Check the change of each wheel speed sensor's value according to the change of vehicle speed.
If the sensor's value is stuck, self-diagnose VDC/ESC ECM with scantool. So if there are any DTCs related to the sensor, inspect it at first and check if the DTCs have been erased.
In a case that there is no DTC, select "RPM" item among the service data.
Check the change of the sensor's value according to a vehicle's speed. Specification :It varies according to a vehicle's speed. Fig.1) Wheel Speed Velocity(4WD) Fig.2) Wheel Speed Sensor(VDC/ESC)
Is parameter displayed within specifications? W/Harness Inspection 4WD Control

dtcs.description: Battery Sensor fault detected by ECU Many control units have applied to vehicle as it becomes more eletrolnical. These units control their own system based on information from diverse sensors. Therefore necessity of common use of diverse sensor information and importance of stable power supply are gathering strength. Battery sensor is mounted on battery (-) terminal. It transmits battery voltage, current, temperature information to PCM. PCM controls generating voltage by duty cycle based on these signals. PCM sets this code when error bit in LIN messages from battery sensor indicates "1" DTC Detecting Condition Diagnostic Circuit Diagram Engine Control

steps.instruction: Connect GDS to Data Link Connector(DLC).
IG "ON".
Select "DTC" button, and then Press "DTC Status" to check DTC's information from the DTCs menu.
Read "DTC Status" parameter.
Is parameter displayed "Present fault"? W/Harness Inspection Engine Control
